sn12.44:1.2: “*Bhikkhus*, I will teach the origination and the disappearance of the world.
sn12.44:1.3: Listen to that, attend carefully, I will speak.”
sn12.44:2.1: “And what, *bhikkhus*, is the origination of the world?
sn12.44:2.2: Dependent on the eye and forms [*rūpe*], eye-*viññāṇa* arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:2.8: with birth as condition, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, dejection, and despair come to be.
sn12.44:2.9: This, *bhikkhus*, is the origination of the world.
sn12.44:3.1: Dependent on the ear and sounds …
sn12.44:3.2: dependent on the nose and odors …
sn12.44:3.3: dependent on the tongue and tastes …
sn12.44:3.4: dependent on the body and tangibles …
sn12.44:3.5: dependent on the mind and *dhammas*, mind-*viññāṇa* arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:3.7: with birth as condition, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, dejection, and despair come to be.
sn12.44:3.8: This, *bhikkhus*, is the origination of the world.
sn12.44:4.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is the disappearance of the world?
sn12.44:4.2: Dependent on the eye and forms [*rūpe*], eye-*viññāṇa* arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:4.5: From the remainderless fading away and cessation of that very *taṇhā*, cessation of *upādāna*;
sn12.44:4.7: so there is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha* [*dukkhakkhandhassa*].
sn12.44:4.8: This, *bhikkhus*, is the disappearance of the world.
sn12.44:5.1: Dependent on the ear and sounds …
sn12.44:5.10: so there is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha* [*dukkhakkhandhassa*].
sn12.44:5.11: This, *bhikkhus*, is the disappearance of the world.”
sn12.44:5.2: dependent on the nose and odors …
sn12.44:5.3: dependent on the tongue and tastes …
sn12.44:5.4: dependent on the body and tangibles …
sn12.44:5.5: dependent on the mind and *dhammas*, mind-*viññāṇa* arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:5.8: From the remainderless fading away and cessation of that very *taṇhā*, cessation of *upādāna*;
